questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Lithium occurs naturally in two isotopes, \({}^{6}Li\ and\ {}^{7}Li\). It can form lithium nitride \((Li_3N)\) whne burnt in air.
questions.answer: If the candidate gives the answer in the form of a chemical equation, it is not necessary to have the chemical equation correctly balanced.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Calculate the percentage abundance of \({}^{6}Li\) in nature.
children.answer:
\(6.0x +7.0(1-x) =6.9\)
\(x= 0.1 = 10.0%\) (Accept answer without unit)(Accept 0.1, 10, 10.0)

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Draw the electron diagram for lithium nitride, showing electrons in the outermost shells only.
children.answer: [IMG:q1_img_0.png]

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: In an experiment, 1.25 g of lithium nitride is formed when a piece of lithium is burnt in air.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Write a chemical equation for the reaction involved.
children.answer:
\(6Li\ +\ N_2\ \rightarrow\ 2Li_3N\)
(State symbols not required)(Ignore incorrect state symbols)

children.label: ii
children.question_text:
Calculate the mass of lithium that reacted with nitrogen.
(Relative atomic masses: Li = 6.9, N = 14.0)
children.answer:
\( y\ /\ 6.9\ = 3 \times(1.25/34.7)\)
\(y=0.756\ g\) (Also accept 0.745, 0.75; Not accept 0.750) (Correct unit is required)
(Accept max. 4 decimal places)

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Name another compound which will also be formed when lithium is burnt in air.
sub_questions.answer: Lithium oxide / Lithium peroxide

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: This question involves the preparation of ammonia gas and the investigation of the properties of ammonia gas in a laboratory.
questions.answer: The answer should state the reagents and products correctly (including the water formed in the neutralization reaction).

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Solid calcium hydroxide reacts with solid ammonium chloride to form ammonia gas. Draw a labelled diagram to show the set-up involved and how ammonia gas is collected.
sub_questions.answer:
Set-up for preparation - boiling tube with reagents and HEAT (with stopper) 
(Accept heating the reagents in a flask)
Upward delivery of ammonia gas (without stopper) 
(Accept collecting the gas with a gas syringe)

[IMG:ans_q2_a_0.jpg]

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text:
An experiment was performed to investigate the properties of ammonia gas with the set-up shown below :

[IMG:q2_img_0.png]

The round-bottomed flask was initially full of dry ammonia gas. Several drops of water were injected into the flask from the dropper. The water containing phenolphthalein was then automatically sucked into the flask through the glass tube.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Briefly explain why the water containing phenolphthalein was sucked into the flask.
children.answer:
Ammonia is soluble in water / Ammonia reacts with water to form aqueous ammonia. 
As all ammonia dissolves, the atmospheric pressure forces the water in the trough to inject into the flask through the glass tubing / the pressure inside the flask is reduced.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: State, with explanation, an observation related to phenolphthalein in the flask.
children.answer:
The water in the flask turns from colourless to pink. 
It is because aqueous ammonia is alkaline.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.answer:
If the candidate gives the answer in the form of a correct ionic equation, or state the reagents and the products in correct ionic forms, the answer is considered to have correct chemical concept, but failed to state the reagents and products completely. (Maximum) Deduct 1 mark for the whole question.
Example: If the candidate only stated 4 correct ionic equations, but in each of the entries the reagents and the products were not stated explicitly, maximum 3 marks will be awarded for the chemical knowledge.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Explain whether  \( BaCl_{2} \)  or  \( OCl_{2} \)  would have a higher melting point.
sub_questions.answer:
The electrostatic attraction between  \( Ba^{2+} \)  and  \( Cl^{-} \)  in  \( BaCl_{2} \)  is ionic bond while intermolecular attraction between  \( OCl_{2} \)  molecules are van der Waals' forces. /
\( BaCl_{2} \)  is an ionic compound while  \( OCl_{2} \)  has a simple molecular structure.
As ionic bond is much stronger than van der Waals' forces / intermolecular forces between  \( OCl_{2} \)  molecules,  \( BaCl_{2} \)  would have a higher melting point than  \( OCl_{2} \) .

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text:
Explain the following decreasing order of the boiling points of three substances :
\( NH_{3} > PH_{3} > CH_{4} \)
sub_questions.answer:
\( NH_{3} > PH_{3} > CH_{4} \)
• Both molecules of  \( PH_{3} \)  and  \( CH_{4} \)  are held by van der Waals' forces / intermolecular forces.
• The van der Waals' forces between  \( PH_{3} \)  are stronger that those between  \( CH_{4} \)  because of the larger molecular size of  \( PH_{3} \)  than  \( CH_{4} \) .
(Accept:  \( PH_{3} \)  molecule has more electrons than  \( CH_{4} \) ;
Not Accept:  \( PH_{3} \)  has a higher molecular mass than  \( CH_{4} \) )
OR • Intermolecular forces between  \( PH_{3} \)  molecules are stronger than that between  \( CH_{4} \)  (2)
molecules as  \( PH_{3} \)  is polar while  \( CH_{4} \)  is non-polar.
• Hydrogen bond exists among  \( NH_{3} \)  molecules that is stronger than van der Waals' forces.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Draw a three-dimensional diagram to represent the molecular shape of  \( SF_{6} \) .
sub_questions.answer: [IMG:ans_q3_b_0.jpg]

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text: Petroleum is an important source of hydrocarbons.
questions.answer:
The following answers are considered to have the products stated correctly.
\( Na_{2}O(s) + 2HCl(l) \rightarrow 2Na^{+}(aq) + 2Cl^{-}(aq) + H_{2}O(l) \)
\( SO_{2}(g) + 2NaOH(aq) \rightarrow 2Na^{+}(aq) + SO_{3}^{2-}(aq) + H_{2}O(l) \)

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Describe the origin of petroleum.
sub_questions.answer:
• Petroleum is formed when large quantities of dead marine organisms (such as planktons and algae).
• that are buried underneath sedimentary rock and subjected to intense heat and pressure for a long time.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: D, E and F are isomeric alkenes containing four carbon atoms. D and E are cis-trans isomers.
sub_questions.answer:
Any two: 2

Accept :
\[ \begin{array}{c}\mathrm{CH}_{3}\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CHCH}_{3}\\\mathrm{CH}_{3}\mathrm{CH}_{2}\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}_{2}\quad\mathrm{CH}_{3}-\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CH}_{2}\end{array} \]

children.label: i
children.question_text: Draw the structure of E (trans-isomer).
children.answer: [IMG:q4_img_0.png]

children.label: ii
children.question_text: State the systematic name of one possible structure of F.
children.answer: but-1-ene or 2-methylpropene / methylpropene

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Ethene and ethane are hydrocarbons.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Suggest how ethene can be converted to ethane.
children.answer:
Pass excess  \( H_{2} \)  to ethene in the presence of Pt / Pd / Ni. /
Catalytic hydrogenation

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Suggest a chemical test to distinguish between ethane and ethene.
children.answer:
Ethene turns  \( Br_{2} \) (in  \( CH_{3}CCl_{3} \) ), 1
from brown / orange to colourless while ethane does not. (Not accept yellow) 1
(Aceept  \( KMnO_{4}/H^{+} \) - purple to colourless /
\( KMnO_{4} \) - purple to brown (precipitate)
\( KMnO_{4}/OH^{-} \) - purple to brown (precipitate))
(Accept: Combustion test (1); ethene gives more sooty flame, while ethane gives less sooty flame (1))

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Electroplating and rust prevention are common applications of electrochemistry.
questions.answer:
(a)

• All 3 labels correct: 2 marks
• Any 1 label correct: 1 mark
(Accept drawing of battery with correct poles / only + and – signs at the correct positions / electron flows in correct direction in the external circuit.)

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text:
The diagram below shows an incomplete set-up. Add suitable drawings and labels to the diagram for electroplating of silver onto the knife.

[IMG:q5_img_0.png]
sub_questions.answer:
[IMG:q5_img_1.png]

All 3 labels correct: 2 marks
Any 1 label correct: 1 mark
(Accept drawing of battery with correct poles / only + and - signs at the correct positions / electron flows in correct direction in the external circuit.)

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest a method, besides painting or electroplating, that can prevent underground iron-made pipelines from rusting. Explain your answer.
sub_questions.answer:
• Connect zinc/magnesium blocks (through connecting wires) to the surface of the pipelines. / Sacrificial protection.
- Zinc/Magnesium can release electrons more readily than iron. / Zinc/Magnesium is more reactive than iron. / Zinc/Magnesium has greater reducing power than iron. / Zinc/Magnesium is higher than iron in the ECS.
OR
• Connect the negative electrode of a D.C. source (through connecting wires) to the surface of the pipelines (and the positive electrode to a platinum electrode) / Cathodic protection
• The electrons provided by the D.C. source prevent iron from releasing electrons. (Do not accept wrapping with plastics / alloying / use stainless steel pipelines)

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Glucose ( \( C_{6}H_{12}O_{6} \) ) is one important energy source for living things.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Write a chemical equation for the conversion of carbon dioxide gas and liquid water to solid glucose and oxygen gas.
children.answer: \( 6CO_{2}(g) + 6H_{2}O(l) \rightarrow C_{6}H_{12}O_{6}(s) + 6O_{2}(g) \)  (Ignore state symbols)

children.label: ii
children.question_text:
The following standard enthalpy changes of formation are given :

\(  CO_{2}(g)=-394\ kJ\ mol^{-1},H_{2}O(l)=-286\ kJ\ mol^{-1},C_{6}H_{12}O_{6}(s)=-1274\ kJ\ mol^{-1} \)

Calculate the standard enthalpy change of the conversion in (i) above.

children.label: iii
children.question_text: Green plants can convert carbon dioxide and water to glucose and oxygen. State the transformation of energy in this conversion.
children.answer: Light / solar energy changes to chemical energy.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text:
Burning heptane ( \( C_{7}H_{16} \) ) releases energy. The enthalpy change of combustion of heptane was determined using the set-up shown below:

[IMG:q6_img_0.png]

Step (I) : The aluminium can with a fixed mass of water was heated by burning methanol. The temperature of water increased by 18.5 °C after 1.58 g of methanol was burnt.
Step (II) : The aluminium can with the same mass of water in Step (I) was heated by burning heptane. The temperature of water increased by  \( 25.8^{\circ} \) C after 1.02 g of heptane was burnt.

children.label: i
children.question_text:
Given that, under the conditions of experiment, the enthalpy change of combustion of methanol is -715 kJ mol \( ^{-1} \) , calculate the enthalpy change of combustion of heptane, in kJ mol \( ^{-1} \) , under the same conditions.

(Relative molecular masses: methanol = 32.0, heptane = 100.0)
children.answer:
Let C be the heat capacity of the calorimeter,
\( -715 \times (1.58 / 32.0) = -C \times 18.5 \ldots (1) \) 
\( \Delta H \times (1.02 / 100.0) = -C \times 25.8 \ldots (2) \)   
\( \Delta H = -4826.8 \, kJ \, mol^{-1} \)  (Accept -4823 to -4831.1) 
Accept  \( \Delta H \times m/M = C \times \Delta T \)  as an alternative to (1).

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Besides heat loss, suggest another source of error in the experiment.
children.answer: Incomplete combustion. / Some methanol or heptane evaporates.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: An experiment was performed to determine the number of water of crystallisation, n, in a sample of hydrated sodium tetraborate  \( \left(\mathrm{Na}_{2}\mathrm{B}_{4}\mathrm{O}_{7} \cdot \mathrm{nH}_{2}\mathrm{O}\right) \) . 0.452 g of the sample was dissolved completely in about  \( 50 \, cm^{3} \)  of deionised water in an apparatus X. The solution obtained was alkaline and was immediately titrated in X with 0.125 M HCl(aq) using methyl orange as an indicator. It required  \( 18.98 \, cm^{3} \)  of the acid to reach the end point.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Name X.
sub_questions.answer: conical flask  \( \dagger \)

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: State the colour change at the end point of the titration.
sub_questions.answer: yellow to orange (Do not accept red)

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text:
It is known that in the reaction during the titration, the mole ratio of  \( B_{4}O_{7}^{2-}(aq) \)  to  \( H^{+}(aq) \)  is 1:2. Calculate the number of water of crystallisation, n.
(Relative atomic masses: H = 1.0, B = 10.8, O = 16.0, Na = 23.0)

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: It is known that hydrated sodium tetraborate can be used to prepare standard solutions.

children.label: i
children.question_text: What is meant by the term ‘standard solutions’?
children.answer: Solutions with accurately known concentrations.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Suggest one use of standard solutions.
children.answer: It can be used to determine the concentration of another reagent / number of water of crystallization / molar mass, etc. via titration / to prepare a calibration curve.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Refer to the experimental set-up as shown below.

[IMG:q8_img_0.png]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: HCl is a strong acid. What is meant by the term ‘strong acid’?
sub_questions.answer: An acid which can (almost) completely ionize/ ionise / highly ionise / dissociate to  \( H^{+} \)  ions in water.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: When concentrated HCl(aq) is dropped into KMnO_{4}(s), a yellowish green gas is formed.

children.label: i
children.question_text: What is the yellowish green gas?
children.answer: chlorine / \(Cl_{2}(g) \)

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Explain whether the reaction forming the yellowish green gas is a redox reaction.
children.answer: It is a redox reaction: O.N. of Cl changes from -1 to 0 / of Mn changes from +7 to +2 / Cl\( ^{-} \) transfers electrons to MnO\( _{4}^{-} \)  / O.N. of Mn and Cl change at the same time / MnO\( _{4}^{-} \)  is reduced and Cl\( ^{-} \) is oxidised.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: With the aid of an ionic equation, state the expected observation when the yellowish green gas reaches the filter paper.
sub_questions.answer:
The filter paper turns yellowish brown. (Do not accept yellow / orange) 
\( 2I^{-} + Cl_{2} \rightarrow 2Cl^{-} + I_{2} \)  (Ignore state symbols)

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: In consideration of laboratory safety, explain where the experiment should be performed.
sub_questions.answer:
The experiment should be performed in a fume cupboard as chlorine gas is toxic / toxic gas is released.
(Do not accept well-ventilated benches, etc.)

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text: Tetrafluoroethene undergoes polymerisation to form a polymer called 'Teflon'. Using this example, describe this type of polymerisation.
questions.answer:
Five knowledge points (1 mark for each point), a maximum of 4 marks: 
• Unsaturated compounds / Compounds with C=C bonds can undergo addition polymerisation.
• No small molecules will be eliminated during addition polymerisation.
• High temp / High Pressure / Catalyst is used. (Any 2 conditions)
• Structure of the monomer :  \( CF_{2}=CF_{2} \)
• Structure of the repeating unit :  \( -CF_{2}-CF_{2}-OR \)  the polymer :  \( -[CF_{2}-CF_{2}]_{n}- \)
Communication mark
(Chemical knowledge = 0 to 2, communication mark = 0,
Chemical knowledge = 3 to 4, communication mark = 0 or 1)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Outline a synthetic route, with no more than three steps, to accomplish the following conversion. For each step, give the reagent(s), reaction conditions (as appropriate) and structure of the organic product.

[IMG:q10_img_0.png]
questions.answer:
(1) \(LiAlH_4\) (2) \(H_3O^+\)
\(HOCH_2CH_2CH_2CH_2OH\)
\(PCl_3\ /\ PCl_5\ /\ HCl\ /\ SOCl_2 \)
(Intermediate: 1 mark; reagent for each step: 1 mark)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following reaction :

\( \mathrm{Br_{2}(aq)+HCO_{2}H(aq)\rightarrow2HBr(aq)+CO_{2}(g)} \)

In an experiment to study the rate of consumption of  \( Br_{2}(aq) \) , equal volumes of 0.01 M  \( Br_{2}(aq) \)  and 1.0 M  \( HCO_{2}H(aq) \)  were mixed. The progress of the reaction was followed by measuring a certain parameter of the reaction system using a colorimeter. The graph below shows the results from the start of the reaction.

[IMG:q11_img_0.png]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Assume that the rate of change of the parameter with time can represent the rate of reaction.

children.label: i
children.question_text: According to the shape of the curve above, suggest what the parameter should be.
children.answer: colour intensity / absorbance (Not accept transmittance)

children.label: ii
children.question_text: The initial rate of the reaction can be determined by a suitable sketch on the above graph. Draw the suitable sketch on the above graph, and describe how the initial rate of the reaction can be obtained from the sketch.
children.answer:
(On the graph) Plot a tangent (a straight line) at time = 0 on the curve.
The initial rate equals to the slope of the tangent / straight line.

children.label: iii
children.question_text: According to the graph above, the rate of reaction at A is higher than that at B. Explain this at molecular level.
children.answer:
The absorbance is (directly) proportional to  \( [Br_{2}(aq)] \)  / number of  \( Br_{2} \)  molecules in the reaction mixture. /
The  \( [Br_{2}(aq)] \)  / number of  \( Br_{2}\) molecules in the reaction mixture at A is higher than that at B,
therefore the frequency of (effective) collisions between molecules at A is higher than that at B.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest another method that can follow the progress of the reaction.
sub_questions.answer: Measure the volume of  \( CO_{2} \)  gas formed (at different time). / Measure the (total) pressure of the system (at different time). (the reaction proceeds in a closed system) / Measure the mass of the reaction mixture (at different time). (Not accept measuring the pH of the reaction mixture)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Aspirin is a pain-killer. Its structure is shown below:

[IMG:q12_img_0.png]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: State one medical application of aspirin other than pain-killing.
sub_questions.answer: Reduce fever / inflammation / risk of heart attack / Rheumatoid arthritis (Not accept hypertension)

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Explain why a suspension of aspirin and water can become clear when sodium hydrogencarbonate powder is added.
sub_questions.answer:
-COOH group of aspirin reacts with hydrogencarbonate ions in water to give a soluble sodium salt / soluble ions / soluble -COO⁻. (Not accept soluble substance / soluble compound)
(Accept: RCOOH  \( \xrightarrow{HCO_{3}^{-}} \)  soluble RCOO⁻ / RCOO⁻ (aq))

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Heating aspirin with excess dilute aqueous acid under reflux will give two organic products.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Draw the structures of these two organic products.
children.answer:
[IMG:ans_q12_i_0.jpg]

[IMG:ans_q12_i_1.jpg]

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Explain why the conversion of aspirin to these two organic products can hardly reach 100% even though the mixture of aspirin and dilute acid is heated under reflux for a long time.
children.answer:
Hydrolysis of ester in acidic medium is a reversible reaction /
and if the reaction mixture is heated under reflux for a long time, it attains equilibrium position and reactants and products co-exist in the system.

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text:
Ibuprofen is also a pain-killer. Its structure is shown below :

[IMG:q12_img_1.png]

There exists enantiomerism in ibuprofen. Draw the three-dimensional structures for the pair of enantiomers.
sub_questions.answer:
[IMG:q12_img_2.png]

Note:
1 mark for the correct spatial arrangements of the chiral centers of the two enantiomers.
1 mark for the correct structures of the four substituents connected to the chiral center.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
An experiment was performed for a reversible reaction involving  \( X(g) \) ,  \( Y(g) \)  and  \( Z(g) \)  in a closed container of  \( 2.0 \, dm^3 \)  at a constant temperature. The graph below shows the relevant experimental data.

[IMG:q13_img_0.png]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: According to the graph, how do you know that the reaction is reversible?
sub_questions.answer:
None of the final concentrations of X(g), Y(g) and Z(g) is equal to zero. / X, Y, Z co-exist in the system, and their concentrations remain unchanged after a long period of time. /
The concentration of the reactant, Y, is still not equal to zero after a long period of time.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Calculate the equilibrium constant  \( K_{e} \)  for the reaction at the temperature of the experiment.
sub_questions.answer:
2Y(g) ⇌ 3X(g) + Z(g) (1 mark for correct equation or Kc expression) 
\( K_{c} = [X(g)]^{3} [Z(g)] / [Y(g)]^{2} \)
\( =(0.60)^{3} (0.20) / (0.30)^{2} \)  (1 mark for correct final concentrations of X, Y and Z, and substituting the numbers into the expression)
\( = 0.48 \, mol^{2} \, dm^{-6} \)  (Correct unit is required) (Not accept  \( M^{2} \) )

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text:
Comment on the following statement :

'The rate of the forward reaction is zero at the  \( 25^{th} \)  minute after the start of the reaction.'
sub_questions.answer:
The statement is INCORRECT.
At the  \( 25^{th} \)  minute after the reaction has started, the reaction attained dynamic equilibrium. /
The rate of forward reaction is equal to the rate of backward reaction, and both of the rates are not equal to zero.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Using  \( Na_{2}O \) ,  \( Al_{2}O_{3} \)  and  \( SO_{2} \)  as examples, illustrate the acid-base behaviour of the oxides of the third period elements with the aid of relevant reactions.
questions.answer:
•  \( Na_{2}O(s) \)  dissolves in water to give NaOH(aq) /
\( Na_{2}O(s) \)  reacts with HCl(aq) to give NaCl(aq) and  \( H_{2}O \)  (or similar reactions) /
\( Na_{2}O(s) + H_{2}O(l) \rightarrow 2NaOH(aq) / \)
\( Na_{2}O(s) + 2HCl(l) \rightarrow 2NaCl(aq) + H_{2}O(l) \)
\( Al_{2}O_{3}(s) \)  reacts with HCl(aq) to give  \( AlCl_{3}(aq) \)  and  \( H_{2}O \)  (or similar reactions) /
\( Al_{2}O_{3}(s) + 6HCl(aq) \rightarrow 2AlCl_{3}(aq) + 3H_{2}O(l) \)
•  \( Al_{2}O_{3}(s) \)  reacts with NaOH(aq) to give  \( NaAl(OH)_{4}(aq) \)  (or similar reactions) /
\( Al_{2}O_{3}(s) \)  + 2NaOH(aq) + 3H_{2}O(l) → 2NaAl(OH)_{4}(aq) /
\( Al_{2}O_{3}(s) \)  + 2NaOH \( (aq) \)  → 2Na \( [AlO_{2}]_{(aq)} \)  + H_{2}O(l)
(At the reactant side, accept NaOH(aq) / NaOH solution without explicitly mentioning water)
•  \( SO_{2}(g) \)  dissolves in water to give  \( H_{2}SO_{3}(aq) \) /
\( SO_{2}(g) \)  reacts with NaOH(aq) to give  \( Na_{2}SO_{3}(aq) \)  and  \( H_{2}O \)  (or  \( SO_{2}(g) \)  reacts with  \( SO_{2}(g) \)  +  \( H_{2}O(l) \) ) →  \( H_{2}SO_{3}(aq) \) /
\( SO_{2}(g) + 2NaOH(aq) \rightarrow Na_{2}SO_{3}(aq) + H_{2}O(l) \)
• Able to mention  \( Na_{2}O \)  is a basic (alkaline) oxide,  \( Al_{2}O_{3} \)  is an amphoteric oxide, and  \( SO_{2} \)  is an acidic oxide.
• Communication mark
(Chemical knowledge = 0 to 3, communication mark = 0
Chemical knowledge = 4 to 5, communication mark = 0 or 1
Incomplete answer or difficult to understand, communication mark = 0)
